- Design the page to match the rest of your company pages
- Include aesthetically pleasing graphics and photos that match the tone of your other emails
- Incorporate company branding images and copy to stay consistent with your brand voice
[...]
An order tracking page is an (almost) empty canvas. Go beyond the essential details by supplementing the page with content that will be useful or educational to your customer. Fill that empty space with one, two, or all of these bonus features:
[...]
- CTAs for exclusive discounts, seasonal sales, or last chance offers
- A place to review products or submit feedback (used in tandem with post-purchase emails)
- “Frequently bought together” upsell and cross-sell product recommendations
- Abandoned cart notifications for anything not purchased from last time or saved in the customers Wish List
- Customer recommendations for additional products
- FAQs or educational information about how to use the product
- Any PSAs, like delivery delays or how to reach customer support if there’s any issues
[...]
The important thing to keep in mind is that a great order tracking page is not meant to be a static brochure. They are meant to inform, educate, and provide good service. When done effectively, they can also increase sales and repurchases.
[...]
The best order tracking pages do more than show an ETA — they ease customer anxiety, cut down on “where’s my order?” tickets, and pull shoppers back into your brand experience.
[...]
When done right, they’re not just cost savers. They’re revenue drivers. Tracking pages can fuel upsells, cross-sells, and repeat orders — turning what used to be a support burden into a sales channel.
[...]
Design matters here — but it’s not just about making things pretty. As Steve Jobs said, “good design is how it works.” Focus on pages that are on brand, functional, and built for transparency.
[...]
A solid order tracking page is a key component of a best-in-class post-purchase experience. By ensuring that your shoppers are engaged and confident from the first brand interaction to the last, you’ll be able to generate long-term brand loyalty that will keep customers coming back again and again.",
        "position": 8
      },
      {
        "url": "https://ecomdesignpro.com/order-tracking-ux-wismo-reduction/",
        "title": "Order Tracking Page UX That Cuts WISMO Tickets In 2026 - Ecom Design Pro | E-Commerce SEO & Design Tips",
        "description": "In 2026, order tracking page UX, a core component of the broader post-purchase experience, is one of the fastest ways to reduce WISMO tickets (Where Is My Order) and ease ecommerce support burdens. When tracking is vague, customers open a ticket “just in case.” When it’s clear, specific, and action-ready, many never need support.
[...]
This guide breaks down the page modules for real-time tracking, microcopy, and KPIs that consistently cut WISMO volume, while staying mobile-first, privacy-safe, and accessible.
[...]
The goal is simple: reduce “interpretation work.” Your tracking page should explain what the system knows, what it doesn’t know yet, and what the customer can do next to enable self-service tracking and enhance the post-purchase experience.
[...]
In 2026, real-time tracking defines high-performing order status pages that shape the post-purchase experience and maximize ROI of AI. A well-designed order status page reads like a clear timeline, not a mystery novel. Start with the essentials, then progressively disclose detail.
[...]
Use plain-language stages (Ordered, Packed, Shipped, Out for delivery, Delivered), but tie them to scan-confirmed events from the shipping carrier. If you’re between scans, say so.
[...]
- Start a claim / report an issue
[...]
- Reduced-motion support for animated timelines
- Keyboard focus states and readable headings for screen readers
- High-contrast status colors plus icons (don’t rely on color alone)
- Large tap targets and sticky “Need help?” entry
[...]
If your tracking page only works when everything goes right, it won’t cut WISMO tickets. Delivery exceptions drive the highest contact spikes, so design them like a product flow, not a support afterthought, boosting customer loyalty in the long run. Managing multiple vendors requires robust multi-carrier logic to handle these delivery exceptions correctly.
[...]
Show a visible “Delay detected” state when your rules trigger (for example, missed delivery window, no scans for X hours, or carrier exception codes).
[...]
Don’t expose full addresses on a shared screen. Instead, confirm identity and allow edits safely.
[...]
To keep improvements honest and prove the ROI of AI (such as AI chatbots) and UX investments, connect tracking UX to support outcomes. Instrument the page like a product surface, not a static receipt.
[...]
- Self-service tracking: Build a branded tracking page where the order status page serves as the central hub, summarizing with an action-first approach, integrating proactive notifications to empower customers and minimize support needs.
[...]
- Proactive notifications: Offer automated notifications via SMS notifications, WhatsApp alerts, and email opt-ins, and don’t spam duplicates.
[...]
- Self-service first: Change delivery, update address (guarded), report issue, start return.
[...]
- Exception states: Delay, address issue, and lost package each have next actions and clear SLAs.
[...]
- Timeline clarity: Self-service tracking ensures status labels match carrier reality, including “label created” and scan gaps. Include tracking links sent via email for easy, immediate access.
[...]
A good tracking page, powered by a self-service portal, doesn’t just report shipment status; it reduces uncertainty, gives customers control, and builds customer loyalty. When order tracking page UX combines clear scan-based updates, delivery exceptions, self-service tracking, and safe self-service actions with proactive notifications and automated notifications, WISMO drops and effort scores improve. These UX improvements through post-purchase orchestration are especially critical during peak season to prevent ecommerce support backlogs. Pick two friction points this week, then measure call deflection and repeat contacts for 30 days. Reducing WISMO tickets shows when customers stop asking “Where is my order?”; it’s not luck, it’s design. Order Tracking Page UX That Cuts WISMO Tickets In 2026.",
        "position": 9
      },
      {
        "url": "https://www.shoplazza.com/blog/practices-for-order-tracking-optimization",
        "title": "10 Strategies to Optimize Ecommerce Order Tracking (2026)",
        "description": "Learn 10 proven ecommerce order tracking strategies to improve
[...]
In ecommerce, paying for an order doesn't mean the shopping experience ends. The time from placing the order to receiving the package is just as important. Customers check tracking updates constantly, and if information is unclear or hard to access, it often leads to more customer service questions and frustration. For sellers, this adds extra work and can hurt trust in the brand. A clear and easy-to-use ecommerce order tracking system keeps customers informed, reduces support requests, and makes fulfillment smoother. Here are 10 practical strategies to improve order tracking, boost customer experience, and cut service costs.
[...]
Confirmation emails are just as important. They should include the order number, item details, shipping address, delivery method, payment method, and a tracking link or instructions. Some sellers also note that a tracking link will be sent once the package ships, setting expectations in advance. Clear confirmation pages and emails reduce misunderstandings and make the ecommerce order status easy for customers to follow, lowering support requests.
[...]
Traditional tracking often relies on the carrier's website, where customers must copy their tracking number and jump between sites. This is cumbersome and can break the connection between your brand and the customer. Many sellers now create branded self-service tracking pages so users can check order status directly on the store's website.For example, Track718 offers a tracking plugin that can be embedded into your store. Customers can see shipping updates without leaving your site. Track718 integrates information from over 800 carriers, including FedEx, DHL, UPS, DPD, USPS, and China Post, making it ideal for cross-border sellers. Some merchants also use 17Track's tracking pages. Customers simply enter their tracking number to view package location and progress. This keeps the experience on your site, maintains brand visuals, and boosts both transparency and trust.
[...]
Shipping transparency plays a big role in customer experience. Many disputes aren't about delivery delays but unclear information. For example, if an order stays "shipped" for a long time with no updates, customers may assume something went wrong.To improve transparency, focus on two things. First, show detailed shipping stages: order confirmed, packed, handed to carrier, in transit, customs, out for delivery, and delivered. This is much clearer than just "shipped → delivered" and helps customers know exactly where their package is.Second, set realistic delivery expectations. Show estimated delivery times like "3–7 business days" and note possible delays during peak periods. Some platforms also indicate "on schedule" or "expected 1-day delay" directly on the tracking page. Clear updates reduce guesswork and cut down on customer service inquiries.
[...]
Customers don't check tracking pages every day, so proactive notifications are key. Automated updates can be sent through email, SMS, app push, or chat tools, keeping users informed about their order status in real time.The key is to be timely without overloading customers. Too few notifications can make users think their order isn't moving, while too many can cause fatigue. Many brands integrate shipping updates into automated marketing systems, so notifications are triggered based on user actions. For example, if a customer clicks the tracking link in an email, the system stops sending duplicate alerts.
[...]
A branded order tracking page lets customers check their shipment directly on your website without being redirected to the carrier's site. This keeps the visual identity consistent, improves user experience, increases time spent on your site, and builds trust. Branded pages can also show delivery instructions, customer service links, or recommended products, reducing WISMO inquiries and keeping customers engaged during the shipping process.",
        "position": 10
      }
    ]
  }
}
Now let me synthesize all this research into a comprehensive report.

{"path": "/root/.hermes/hermes-agent/el-viajero-ecommerce-features-research.md", "content": "# El Viajero - E-Commerce Feature Gap Analysis & Research Report
## Best-in-Class Features for LATAM SMB E-Commerce Stores

**Date:** 2026-05-08
**Context:** El Viajero is an outdoor/camping e-commerce store in Paraguay.
**Current Capabilities:** Basic cart, checkout, user accounts, blog, admin panel, WhatsApp integration, newsletter, promo codes, product reviews, recently viewed, compare products, exit intent popup, cookie consent, dark mode, currency toggle (PYG/USD), language toggle (ES/EN/GN).
**Payments:** Not yet live.

---

## 1. ABANDONED CART RECOVERY — CRITICAL GAP

### Industry Context
- Global average cart abandonment: **70.19%** (Baymard Institute 2025)
- Mobile abandonment: **85.2%**; Desktop: **66.3%**
- **55%** abandon due to unexpected extra costs; **25%** due to forced account creation
- Top performers recover **18-22%** of abandoned carts via email sequences

### Best-in-Class Features
- **Multi-email sequences (3 emails):** 1hr post-abandonment (reminder), 24hrs (social proof), 72hrs (incentive/last chance)
- **Multi-channel recovery:** Email + SMS + Web push notifications
- **SMS recovery:** 90%+ open rates within 3 minutes; 21-30% conversion rates
- **Personalization:** Include cart contents, product images, customer name
- **Incentives:** Free shipping threshold (preferred), 10-15% discount on 3rd email only
- **Abandoned cart analytics:** Track recovery rate, revenue recovered, ROI
- **Guest checkout:** Top abandonment cause — must offer guest checkout

### El Viajero Status: ❌ MISSING
No abandoned cart recovery mechanism exists. This is the single highest-ROI feature they could add, potentially recovering 10-20% of lost sales.

### LATAM-Specific Recommendation
- Integrate **WhatsApp-based cart recovery** (already have WhatsApp integration — massive advantage)
- Send first reminder via email, second via WhatsApp with direct checkout link
- Offer PYG-based free shipping thresholds as incentive

---

## 2. ORDER TRACKING UX — CRITICAL GAP

### Industry Standards
- **88% of users** won't return after a poor delivery experience
- **56% of e-commerce sites** don't integrate tracking properly (Baymard 2024)
- Branded tracking pages reduce WISMO ("Where Is My Order?") tickets by **40-60%**

### Best-in-Class Features
- **Real-time tracking** with scan-confirmed events (Ordered → Packed → Shipped → Out for Delivery → Delivered)
- **Branded tracking page** (not carrier's site) — keeps brand engagement, enables upselling
- **Multi-carrier support** — especially critical for Paraguay/regional carriers
- **Proactive notifications** via email, SMS, WhatsApp at each milestone
- **Estimated delivery dates (EDD)** — accurate, updated in real-time
- **Self-service options:** Change delivery, report issue, start return from tracking page
- **Cross-sell/upsell on tracking page** — high-intent visitors
- **Notifications in local languages** (ES/GN already supported)

### El Viajero Status: ❌ MISSING
No order tracking UX mentioned. Basic order status likely lives only in admin panel.

---

## 3. LOYALTY PROGRAM — CRITICAL GAP

### Industry Context
- LATAM loyalty market CAGR: **13.2%** (2022-2026)
- **70% of consumers** say loyalty programs influence purchase decisions
- Acquiring new customers costs **5-25x** more than retaining existing ones

### Best-in-Class Features
- **Points-based programs** (earn points per purchase, redeem for discounts/free products)
- **Tiered/VIP programs** (Silver/Gold/Platinum with escalating benefits)
- **Referral programs** (reward customers for bringing new shoppers)
- **Gamification** (badges, challenges, social sharing)
- **Cashback programs** (instant rebate on next purchase)
- **Earn points for actions:** Reviews, social shares, account creation, birthdays
- **Integration with email/SMS/WhatsApp** for reward notifications

### LATAM-Specific Considerations
- **Puntos Paraguay** or custom points program
- Tiered loyalty based on PYG spending levels
- WhatsApp-based loyalty notifications (leverage existing integration)

### El Viajero Status: ❌ MISSING
No loyalty/rewards program exists. Newsletter and promo codes are present but no structured loyalty mechanics.

---

## 4. BULK PRICING / B2B FEATURES — MISSING (MODERATE GAP)

### Industry Context
- B2B e-commerce in LATAM is growing rapidly
- Outdoor/camping gear has natural B2B demand (schools, tour operators, corporate retreats, resellers)

### Best-in-Class Features
- **Custom price lists per customer group**
- **Volume/quantity-based pricing** (tiered discounts by quantity)
- **Hidden prices** for non-logged-in B2B users
- **Minimum order quantities**
- **Net payment terms / credit lines** for B2B customers
- **Quick reorder / saved carts** for repeat B2B purchases
- **Quote request system** for negotiated pricing
- **Separate B2B portal or dual B2B/B2C mode**

### El Viajero Status: ❌ MISSING
Basic promo codes exist but no structured B2B pricing, no customer group segmentation, no volume discounts.

---

## 5. INVENTORY MANAGEMENT — MODERATE GAP

### Industry Context
- Poor inventory management leads to overselling, stockouts, lost revenue
- Real-time accuracy critical for growing operations

### Best-in-Class Features
- **Real-time inventory tracking** across all products and variants
- **Low-stock alerts and auto-reorder points**
- **Barcode scanning** support
- **Inventory valuation** (FIFO/LIFO/Average)
- **Purchase order management**
- **Supplier management**
- **Inventory analytics** (stock velocity, turnover rates)

### El Viajero Status: ⚠️ PARTIAL/LIKELY BASIC
Basic inventory likely exists in the backend, but no evidence of multi-location, real-time syncing, or advanced inventory features.

---

## 6. PAYMENT GATEWAY OPTIMIZATION — CRITICAL (NOT YET LIVE)

### Industry Context — LATAM Payment Landscape
- **Brazil:** Pix processes more daily transactions than Visa + Mastercard combined; **essential**
- **Mexico:** OXXO serves ~30-40% of online shoppers; cash-based
- **Argentina:** Mercado Pago more trusted than traditional banks
- Offering local payment methods can increase conversion by **up to 30%**

### Best-in-Class Features for Paraguay
| Payment Method | Status | Priority |
|---|---|---|
| Mercado Pago (credit/debit/wallet) | Common in Paraguay | P0 - Essential |
| Credit/debit cards (Visa, MC) | Standard | P0 - Essential |
| Bank transfer / deposit | Common in PY | P1 - Important |
| Pago Express / local processors | Regional | P1 - Important |
| PayPal | Available | P1 - Important |
| Cash on delivery | Outdoor goods | P2 - Consider |
| Cryptocurrency (USDT/BTC) | Growing in PY | P2 - Differentiator |

### Optimization Best Practices
- **Transparent checkout** (customer doesn't leave the store for payment)
- **One-click checkout** (Shop Pay / saved payment methods)
- **Buy Now, Pay Later (BNPL)** — increases conversion by 78% on larger purchases
- **Installments without credit card** (Mercado Credito)
- **Payment method ordering** by customer location
- **3DS authentication** for fraud prevention
- **Show total cost early** — unexpected costs cause 55% abandonment
- **Accept local currency (PYG)** settlement

### El Viajero Status: ❌ NOT YET LIVE
Payments are the single biggest blocker. No payment gateway integrated at all.

---

## 7. SHIPPING CALCULATOR — MODERATE GAP

### Industry Context
- **63% of cart abandonment** due to unexpected shipping costs
- Real-time shipping rates reduce this friction significantly

### Best-in-Class Features
- **Real-time carrier rate fetching** (via carrier API)
- **Weight and dimension-based calculations**
- **Shipping zone management** (domestic/regional PY, international)
- **Free shipping threshold** (e.g., "Free shipping over Gs. 500,000")
- **Multiple shipping options** (standard, express, pickup)
- **Estimated delivery dates** displayed before checkout
- **Address validation/autocomplete**
- **Shipping cost on product page** (not just checkout)

### LATAM-Specific
- Support for regional carriers in Paraguay
- Calculate rates based on PYG logistics zones
- Offer pickup at physical store if applicable

### El Viajero Status: ❌ MISSING
No shipping calculator mentioned. Unclear if basic flat rate or no shipping logic exists.

---

## 8. REAL-TIME STOCK SYNCING — MODERATE GAP

### Industry Standards
- Event-driven sync (under **5 seconds**) is now best-in-class
- Prevents overselling and stock drift across channels
- Essential if selling on multiple platforms (Mercado Libre, Facebook, etc.)

### Best-in-Class Features
- **Bidirectional sync** between store and physical inventory
- **Safety stock buffers** per channel
- **Oversell protection** (reservation systems, race condition prevention)
- **Multi-location stock visibility** in real-time
- **Integration with POS** if physical store exists

### El Viajero Status: ⚠️ UNCERTAIN
Likely basic or no real-time sync if payments aren't live yet.

---

## 9. MOBILE APP / PWA FEATURES — SIGNIFICANT GAP

### Industry Context — LATAM
- **+27% conversion increase** from PWA implementation (Garbarino, Argentina)
- **35% increase in pageviews per session** with PWA
- LATAM mobile app sessions grew **20% YoY** in Q1 2026
- Mobile cart abandonment: **85.2%** vs desktop **66.3%**
- PWA is ideal for LATAM due to limited device storage, bandwidth concerns

### Best-in-Class Features
- **Progressive Web App (PWA):** Installable on homescreen, works offline
- **Add to Home Screen prompt** (timed at key moments)
- **Offline browsing** of previously viewed products
- **Push notifications** (even without native app)
- **App-like experience** on mobile (full-screen, no browser chrome)
- **Splash screens and icons** matching brand
- **Fast loading on 3G/4G networks** (LATAM reality)

### El Viajero Status: ❌ MISSING
No mention of PWA, mobile app, or push notifications. Given LATAM's mobile-first usage patterns, this is a significant gap.

---

## 10. PUSH NOTIFICATIONS — GAP

### Industry Standards
- Web push notifications: **CTR up to 10.36%** when behaviorally targeted
- E-commerce push notification optimal frequency: **1-2 per day**
- Critical for: cart recovery, price drop alerts, back-in-stock, order updates

### Best-in-Class Use Cases
- **Abandoned cart reminders** via push
- **Back-in-stock alerts**
- **Price drop notifications**
- **Order status updates** (shipped, delivered)
- **Flash sale / promotion alerts**
- **Behavioral segmentation** (RFM-based push targeting)

### El Viajero Status: ❌ MISSING
No push notification capability mentioned.

---

## 11. CUSTOMER SEGMENTATION — MAJOR GAP

### Industry Standards
- RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) segmentation is table stakes
- AI-powered segmentation is now available (Klaviyo AI, Omnisend AI)
- Segments automatically update in real-time based on behavior

### Best-in-Class Segments
- **New subscribers** (no purchase yet)
- **First-time buyers** (one order — nurture for second purchase)
- **Repeat buyers** (2+ orders — loyalty candidates)
- **VIPs** (top 10% by LTV — special treatment)
- **At risk / churning** (no purchase in 60+ days)
- **Cart abandoners**
- **Browse abandoners** (viewed but didn't add)
- **Geographic segments** (by city/department in Paraguay)
- **Product category affinity** (tents vs. hiking boots vs. camping gear)

### El Viajero Status: ❌ MISSING
No segmentation mentioned. User accounts exist but no behavioral grouping or targeted marketing based on segments.

---

## 12. EMAIL MARKETING AUTOMATION — PARTIAL GAP

### Industry Standards
- **Automated flows** outperform broadcast campaigns significantly
- Best-in-class flows: Welcome series, Abandoned cart, Post-purchase, Win-back, Replenishment reminders

### Best-in-Class Automated Flows
| Flow | Timing | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Welcome series | Immediate + 3 days | Convert new subscribers |
| Abandoned cart | 1hr / 24hr / 72hr | Recover lost sales |
| Post-purchase | Immediate + 7 days | Review request, cross-sell |
| Win-back | 60 days inactive | Reactivate lapsed customers |
| Birthday/anniversary | Annual | Personalized offer |
| Replenishment | Based on product lifecycle | Reorder reminders |
| Browse abandonment | 1-24hrs | Recover viewed products |

### Current Newsletter (Basic)
El Viajero has a newsletter but likely lacks automated behavioral flows, A/B testing, and performance analytics.

### El Viajero Status: ⚠️ PARTIAL
Newsletter exists but no evidence of automated lifecycle flows, behavioral triggers, or advanced email automation.

---

## 13. MULTI-WAREHOUSE MANAGEMENT — LOW PRIORITY GAP

### Best-in-Class Features
- **Unlimited warehouse support**
- **Warehouse-to-warehouse transfers**
- **Location-based fulfillment routing** (ship from nearest warehouse)
- **Separate stock levels per location**
- **Inventory analytics per warehouse**

### El Viajero Status: ❌ MISSING (Low Priority)
Unlikely to need multi-warehouse at current scale, but should be architectable for future growth.

---

## 14. ANALYTICS & REPORTING — MAJOR GAP

### Best-in-Class Features
- **Real-time sales dashboard** (revenue, orders, AOV, conversion rate)
- **Customer analytics** (LTV, repeat purchase rate, churn rate)
- **Product analytics** (best sellers, margin analysis, stock velocity)
- **Marketing analytics** (campaign ROI, channel attribution, segment performance)
- **Funnel analysis** (where customers drop off)
- **Cohort analysis** (retention over time)
- **Inventory reports** (stock levels, reorder alerts, turnover)
- **Custom report builder**

### Key Metrics to Track
- Conversion rate, AOV, Customer LTV
- Cart abandonment rate & recovery rate
- Email/SMS campaign performance (open rate, CTR, revenue)
- Top products, categories, brands
- Customer acquisition cost by channel
- Inventory turnover and stockout rate

### El Viajero Status: ⚠️ BASIC
Basic admin panel present but no evidence of advanced analytics, dashboards, or performance reporting.

---

## PRIORITY SUMMARY

| Priority | Feature | Impact | Effort |
|---|---|---|---|
| P0 - Critical | **Payment Gateway Integration** | Blocking revenue | High |
| P0 - Critical | **Abandoned Cart Recovery** | High (10-20% recovery) | Medium |
| P0 - Critical | **Shipping Calculator** | High (reduces abandonment) | Medium |
| P1 - High | **PWA / Mobile Optimization** | High (LATAM mobile-first) | Medium |
| P1 - High | **Order Tracking UX** | High (reduces support tickets) | Medium |
| P1 - High | **Customer Segmentation** | Enables all marketing | Medium |
| P1 - High | **Email Marketing Automation** | High (lifecycle revenue) | Medium |
| P1 - High | **Push Notifications** | Medium-High (re-engagement) | Low-Medium |
| P2 - Medium | **Loyalty Program** | Medium (retention) | Medium |
| P2 - Medium | **Analytics & Reporting** | Medium (data-driven decisions) | Medium |
| P2 - Medium | **Real-Time Stock Sync** | Medium (accuracy) | Medium |
| P2 - Medium | **Bulk Pricing / B2B** | Medium (new revenue stream) | Medium |
| P3 - Low | **Multi-Warehouse** | Low (future need) | High |
| P3 - Low | **Inventory Management** | Low-Medium (basic exists) | Medium |

---

## QUICK WINS (Low Effort, High Impact)

1. **Abandoned cart emails** via existing newsletter tool — can be set up in days
2. **WhatsApp-based abandoned cart recovery** — already have WhatsApp integration
3. **Guest checkout option** — simple UX change
4. **Free shipping threshold** communicated on cart page
5. **Basic order tracking page** — branded, with email notifications
6. **Mobile-responsive design audit** — ensure existing theme is fully mobile-optimized

## KEY RECOMMENDATIONS FOR NEXT STEPS

1. **Payments first** — integrate Mercado Pago (most widely used in Paraguay/LATAM) + standard card processing
2. **Abandoned cart recovery** — leverage existing newsletter + WhatsApp, implement 3-email + 1-WhatsApp sequence
3. **Shipping calculator** — implement real-time or table-rate calculation based on PYG logistics zones
4. **PWA implementation** — convert existing store to PWA for "app-like" mobile experience + push notifications
5. **Customer segmentation** — implement RFM-based grouping via existing user accounts
6. **Email automation** — build welcome, post-purchase, and win-back flows using existing newsletter infrastructure
"}

## Research Complete - Here's the Summary

### What I Did
Conducted extensive web research across 15+ sources covering best-in-class e-commerce features specifically relevant to LATAM SMB stores. Searched for: abandoned cart recovery stats, LATAM payment methods (Pix, OXXO, Mercado Pago), PWA case studies from LATAM, order tracking UX benchmarks, inventory management systems, customer segmentation tools, email marketing automation platforms, shipping calculator best practices, loyalty programs, and B2B e-commerce features.

### What I Found

**El Viajero currently has:** basic cart/checkout, user accounts, blog, admin panel, WhatsApp integration, newsletter, promo codes, product reviews, recently viewed, compare products, exit intent popup, cookie consent, dark mode, currency toggle (PYG/USD), language toggle (ES/EN/GN).

**Critical Gaps (P0 - Blocking Revenue):**
1. **Payment Gateway** - Not yet live. Mercado Pago is essential for Paraguay/LATAM. Adding local payment methods can increase conversion by up to 30%.
2. **Abandoned Cart Recovery** - Industry avg 70% cart abandonment; best-in-class recovers 18-22%. El Viajero has WhatsApp integration which is a huge advantage here.
3. **Shipping Calculator** - 63% of cart abandonment is due to unexpected shipping costs. No calculator exists.

**Major Gaps (P1 - High Impact):**
- Order Tracking UX (branded tracking pages reduce support tickets 40-60%)
- PWA / Mobile Optimization (Garbarino Argentina saw +27% conversion from PWA)
- Customer Segmentation (RFM-based - enables all targeted marketing)
- Email Marketing Automation (behavioral flows vs. basic newsletter)
- Push Notifications (CTR up to 10.36% when targeted)
- Analytics & Reporting (no advanced dashboards)

**Medium Gaps (P2):**
- Loyalty Program (LATAM loyalty market growing 13.2% CAGR)
- Bulk Pricing / B2B (outdoor gear has natural B2B demand)
- Real-Time Stock Syncing (essential for multi-channel)
- Inventory Management (likely basic)

**Quick Wins using existing infrastructure:** WhatsApp-based cart recovery (already have WhatsApp), abandoned cart emails via existing newsletter, guest checkout option, free shipping threshold on cart page.
